summary: Decent audience recording of a mixed‑quality 1995 Detroit show, highlighted by a scorching Victim > Foolish Heart jam but marred by uneven set and occasional weak songs.
review: The performance is an uneven slice of the Summer ’95 tour – the first half has solid moments like Bertha and Minglewood Blues, while the second set swings between cheesy Ramble On Rose and a monster Victim > Foolish Heart stretch that many reviewers call the night’s high point. The audience (AUD) source is decent but noisy, offering an authentic “live‑in‑the‑arena” feel without the clarity of a soundboard. Overall the show pleases fans who love the jam but leaves others wishing for tighter playing and better mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 3‑4 stars, praising the Victim > Foolish jam while noting recording noise and several weak songs; weighting show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Decent audience capture with noticeable crowd chatter and occasional muffling; not crisp like a soundboard but preserves the arena atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with classics (Bertha, Minglewood Blues) but a few limp numbers; second set uneven until the long Victim > Foolish jam lifts the show, followed by mixed‑quality finishes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – some call his playing hopelessly lost and painful during the Victim > Foolish transition, while others hail his soaring improvisation on that very jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboard) gets praise for a strong All Too Much performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming and space sections are noted as solid and energetic.
